steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The claim says Winnemucca "explicitly indicates when and how she is shifting the focus of the narrative." The quote must directly name (1) that a shift is happening, (2) what she is shifting from and to, and (3) when or how.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Nothing, happened during the day, and after awhile mother told us not to say a word about why we left, for grandpa might get mad with us.": Recounts an event in the narrative; announces no shift in focus. ✗
  • Choice B — "My dear readers, such is the respect my people have for their chiefs.": A general observation addressed to readers; no announcement of a narrative shift. ✗
  • Choice C — "But how can I describe the scene that followed? Some of you, dear readers, can imagine.": A rhetorical question; acknowledges difficulty but does not explicitly state a shift in topic or time. ✗
  • Choice D — "I will now stop writing about myself and family and tribe customs, and tell about the wars, and the causes of the wars. I will jump over about six years.": Names what she is shifting from (self/family/customs), what she is shifting to (the wars), when (now), and how (jumping six years). ✓
That is the correct answer.
